summary: Complete testing of per-recipient email alerts feature. All backend APIs and frontend UI elements working correctly. 11/11 backend tests passed, 7/7 UI interaction tests passed.

minor.issue: Master toggle fields (sold_out_enabled, rooms_available_enabled, etc.) not persisted in DB until POST is called. Currently returns only recipients and updated_at from DB. The helper function get_email_alert_settings() returns defaults but these don't get saved to DB initially.

critical_code_review_comments: Per-recipient email alerts feature is fully functional and backwards compatible with old string format
seed_data_creation: Test emails are auto-cleaned up after each test run
context_for_next_testing_agent: Per-recipient email alerts feature verified working. Backend APIs: GET/POST /api/admin/email-alerts/settings, POST /api/admin/email-alerts/recipients/add, DELETE /api/admin/email-alerts/recipients, PUT /api/admin/email-alerts/recipients/alerts. Frontend: Portal Settings > Email Alert Recipients section has table with checkboxes for each alert type per recipient.

GET /api/admin/email-alerts/settings.returns: Recipients list with per-recipient alert preferences in format {email, alerts[]}

POST /api/admin/email-alerts/recipients/.behavior: Adds new recipient with all 4 alert types enabled by default

PUT /api/admin/email-alerts/recipients/a.behavior: Updates specific recipient's alert preferences, validates alert types

DELETE /api/admin/email-alerts/recipient.behavior: Removes recipient, returns 404 for non-existent emails

elements_verified: Per-recipient checkboxes for Sold Out, Available, Low Avail, Daily
Master alert switches below the table

Backwards Compatibility.note: API correctly handles both old string format and new object format for recipients

test_scenarios_covered: Add recipient with default alerts (all enabled)
